title: Hunt for methane leaks reveals persistent pollution around French oil and gas sites
subhead: In late April, two NGOs inspected around 60 facilities and recorded leaks of the potent greenhouse gas at two-thirds of them. A high-level summit on the urgent need to reduce methane emissions was held in Paris on Monday.

Hantavirus poses low risk to the public, WHO Europe says. What is the disease and where does it come from?
subhead: While three people have died of the disease on a cruise ship in the southern Atlantic, hantavirus presents a low risk to the public, the head of the World Health Organization Europe said Monday.

title: Jacques Garcia, designer of French opulence
subhead: He rose to fame in the 1990s by designing the interior of Hôtel Costes in Paris. Criticized by purists, his taste for antiques and lavish style won him success with wealthy private clients and luxury hotels.

title: Tame Impala lights up Paris with Justice as surprise guests
subhead: The group led by Australian musician Kevin Parker delivered a brilliant performance at the Accor Arena on Sunday, May 3, blending psychedelic rock, pop and electronic music.

title: Saint Pierre and Miquelon, a pioneering example of climate change adaptation
subhead: This year, residents of a town in the French overseas territory off the coast of Canada are set to move into a new village. This relocation, France's first planned effort to move an entire town, is the result of a lengthy process.

subhead: Despite the emergence of several figures within La France Insoumise and his promise to step aside after the 2022 election, in which he gathered 21.95% of the vote, the populist leader announced his candidacy on Sunday.

title: Trump faces a painful political reality, from Iran stalemate to legal setbacks
subhead: Six months ahead of the US midterm elections, the political momentum has turned sharply against President Donald Trump.

title: Swedish government's anti-immigrant escalation sparks backlash among population
subhead: With a few months to go before the September 13 elections, several new reforms are set to further toughen the conditions for migrants and refugees in the country. Minors born in Sweden, individuals who have lived there for decades and whose children are Swedish citizens now face deportation.
